business.headline: Sensex closes 1,677 points lower, Nifty below 23,900 over renewed US-Iran tensionSensex closes 1,677 points lower, Nifty below 23,900 over renewed US-Iran tension

Dalal Street bloodbath: Sensex tanks 1,600 points, more pain ahead for investors?As of 2:42 pm, the sell-off on Dalal Street intensified, with the benchmark BSE Sensex plunging 1,766.68 points, or 2.26%, to 76,414.04, while the NSE Nifty50 tumbled 553.35 points, or 2.27%, to 23,845.35 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp spike in crude oil prices above $78 per barrel and weak global cues triggered broad-based selling across sectors.
RBI backs crypto ban, tax department cites tracking and tax risksIndia has not yet taken a clear stand on cryptocurrencies, with policy discussions continuing for several years.

New EPFO portal is here. What does it mean for your PF account?EPFO has given its digital platform a major upgrade, bringing all key PF services under one roof. But how will this benefit crores of EPF members? Let's have a look.

US-Iran tensions are back: So, is gold going up?Whenever tensions flare up in the Middle East, gold usually grabs investors' attention. But with the US-Iran conflict back in focus, is the yellow metal finally gearing up for a rally?

South Korea enters bear market as AI chip rally falters despite Samsung profitSouth Korea's KOSPI slipped into bear market territory as investors sold AI-linked chip stocks despite Samsung's record profit forecast. The swing reflected growing doubts over whether the AI-led chip boom can sustain current valuations.

ITR filing alert: 7 tax rules every intraday and F&O trader should knowTax filing has become more detailed for intraday and F&O traders this year. Knowing a few key rules before filing your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices and compliance hassles.

The hidden tricks making you spend more online: Why Sebi may finally step inA study found dark patterns on most listed consumer transaction platforms and urged Sebi to seek self-declarations. The report casts deceptive design as both a consumer protection issue and a corporate governance risk.

Name one car damaged by ethanol fuel: Nitin Gadkari dares E20 criticsUnion Minister Nitin Gadkari defended the E20 petrol rollout and challenged critics to name any damaged car. His remarks came as concerns over mileage, older vehicles and a possible move beyond E20 intensified.

Crude oil rally hits Dalal Street: IndiGo, HPCL, BPCL fall up to 5%A rise in crude oil prices after fresh US-Iran tensions dragged aviation and oil marketing stocks lower on Dalal Street. InterGlobe Aviation, the parent company of IndiGo, dropped more than 3%, while SpiceJet also traded lower.

Sensex ends 1,677 points lower: 3 reasons why stock market crashed todayAs of 1:56 pm, selling pressure intensified on Dalal Street, with the benchmark BSE Sensex tumbling 1,122.46 points, or 1.44%, to 77,058.26, while the NSE Nifty50 plunged 342.60 points, or 1.40%, to 24,056.10 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp rise in crude oil prices and weak global cues triggered broad-based risk-off sentiment across equities.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as US-Iran tensions push crude above $76The BSE Sensex was down 535.44 points, or 0.68%, at 77,645.28, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 158.45 points, or 0.65%, to 24,241.00 at around 9:25 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market crash today?Sensex and Nifty are headed for a weak start after the US-Iran flare-up shook global markets and lifted crude prices. GIFT Nifty was trading at 24,246 around 8 am, down 164 points.

Oil rises over 2% to $76 as fresh US-Iran escalation revives supply fearsAs of 8 am, Brent crude was trading at $76.15 a barrel, up 2.68%, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude rose to $72.41 a barrel, gaining 2.80%.

India's gifting habits are getting a digital makeoverNot too long ago, gifting meant wrapping presents with ribbons and colourful paper. Today, a quick scan or a digital voucher is increasingly replacing that familiar tradition.

July 31 vs August 31: Which ITR filing deadline applies to you?The countdown to ITR filing has begun, but this year there's a catch. The familiar one-deadline-for-all approach is gone. Whether you need to file by July 31 or August 31 depends on your taxpayer category and the ITR form you are using.

Markets snap 4-day rally as profit booking offsets IT surge; Trent down 12%Benchmark indices ended slightly lower on Tuesday as profit booking offset strong gains in IT shares. Caution ahead of the US Fed minutes and the earnings season kept sentiment measured, even as the rupee strengthened.

Trent loses Rs 13,000 crore in market value: Why are investors selling?The stock fell as much as 12.33% to Rs 2,931.15 during the day's trade, making it the biggest loser on the Nifty 50 index. By around 1:15 pm, Trent's market capitalisation had dropped to about Rs 97,311 crore from Rs 1.11 lakh crore in the previous session, eroding nearly Rs 13,356 crore in investor wealth in a single day.

Multiple marriages to family businesses: India's new need for wealth managersBuilding wealth is only half the journey. As Indian families become more complex, protecting that wealth and passing it on smoothly is becoming an even bigger challenge.

Government denies reports of E25 petrol rollout, says no decision taken yetGovernment sources have denied reports of an E25 rollout and said no decision has been taken yet. They said E20 has been in safe use after testing and any further move will depend on scientific findings.

entertainment.headline: Internet not impressed with Toxic's Tabaahi song, calls Yash-Kiara's romance flat The romantic song, Tabaahi from Toxic featuring Yash and Kiara Advani, was released on July 8. While many viewers praised its visuals, others questioned the lead pair's on-screen chemistry.

Who plays whom in Satluj? Your guide to people that inspired film's characters Satluj has renewed interest in the real people behind its characters after its brief Zee5 release. The film revisits Jaswant Singh Khalra's case while viewers track how closely each role mirrors real events.

Lock Upp 2: Akanksha Chamola says she won't remarry after divorce from Gaurav Khanna Lock Upp 2 contestant Akanksha Chamola said she does not want to marry again and wants to live on her own terms. Her remarks came after she revealed her separation from Gaurav Khanna and spoke about differing views on parenthood.

Robert Pattinson compares his character from The Odyssey to Twilight's Jacob Black Actor Robert Pattinson said his role as Antinous in director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is a bit like Jacob Black in Twilight. The comparison links his latest film to the love triangle that defined one of his best-known franchises.

Satluj writer reacts to Diljit's film ban, dismisses 'marketing stunt' allegations Satluj writer Niren Bhatt has dismissed claims that the film's removal from ZEE5 was a marketing stunt, saying the makers always wanted an official release. He also urged viewers to wait for the film's return instead of watching pirated copies.

Karavali row explained: Kannada actor Prajwal's no-show, unpaid dues and more What began as Karavali's trailer launch soon turned into a public dispute involving actor Prajwal Devaraj, the makers and a growing fan backlash. The controversy stemmed from pending payments, dubbing no-show and fan protests. Here's what happened.

Actor Ravi Mohan not quitting cinema, apologises to fans amid divorce battle Actor Ravi Mohan has withdrawn his decision to stop acting during his divorce battle with Aarti Ravi. He made the announcement at an award show in Chennai and apologised to his fans.

The Odyssey first reviews: Christopher Nolan's epic praised as crowning achievement Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has received glowing first reactions ahead of its July 17 release, with many calling it the director's finest work. While most praised its scale and visuals, a few viewers felt the epic was slow and drawn out.

Internet calls Rajkummar Rao misfit as Dada Sourav Ganguly; see reactions to poster The first-look poster for actor Rajkummar Rao's upcoming sports biopic, Dada, was unveiled on July 8, drawing immediate reactions across social media. The response was sharply divided between immense excitement for cricketer Sourav Ganguly's life story and intense criticism of the actor's seemingly AI-generated appearance.

Tabaahi song out: Yash, Kiara Advani turn up the heat in Toxic's romantic track The makers of Toxic have released the official video of Tabaahi, pairing Yash and Kiara Advani in an intense romantic track. The video song offers the first entry into the film's world while teasing its wider character dynamics.

Seema Sajdeh confirms Alliance entry, says son pushed her and Sohail for show Seema Sajdeh has confirmed her entry into Prime Video's Alliance, where Sohail Khan is already competing. She said their younger son Yohan pushed both of them to take up the show, while teamwork will define the contest.

Does Satluj promote pro-Khalistan narrative? What Diljit Dosanjh film really says For all the debate around Khalistan, Satluj asks a very different question. Honey Trehan's film refuses to argue ideology, choosing instead to confront the unresolved human cost of Punjab's counter-insurgency years and the accountability they continue to demand.

Satluj row shocks actor Kanwaljit Singh after film's takedown from Zee5 India Kanwaljit Singh has questioned the fear around Satluj after its brief release and removal in India. His remarks also draw attention to the film's long censorship battle and uncertain status.

Ahmed Khan defends 8-hour work shifts, says filmmakers should plan better Director Ahmed Khan addressed the debate around eight-hour work shifts in the film industry, saying the change should be accepted and schedules should be planned accordingly. He also revealed that Welcome to the Jungle was completed in 75 eight-hour shifts while staying within budget.

Rajkummar Rao recreates Sourav Ganguly's iconic shirtless moment in Dada first look The makers of Dada - The Sourav Ganguly Story unveiled Rajkummar Rao's first look as Sourav Ganguly on the former captain's birthday. The poster recreates Ganguly's Lord's balcony celebration, underscoring the film's focus on his impact on Indian cricket.

Sex over violence any day: Toxic actor defends new teaser, rejects criticism Actor Benedict Garrett has responded to the backlash over Toxic's Ladies and Ladies teaser during an Instagram question and answer session. His remarks have added to the debate around the promo's sensual imagery, violence and portrayal of women.

Who is Honey Trehan, once Vishal Bhardwaj's right hand, now director of Satluj? Honey Trehan, a popular casting director turned filmmaker, is known for his close collaboration with Vishal Bhardwaj. His latest film Satluj, centred on human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, has sparked fresh debates on censorship and artistic freedom in India.

Anurag Kashyap urges people to watch Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj 'even if pirated' Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap reacted to the removal of Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj from ZEE5. The filmmaker urged people to watch the film, even if they had to rely on pirated copies.

Sikh body to hold Satluj public screenings and seminars after ZEE5 takedown The Delhi Sikh Gurdwara Management Committee (DSGMC) has opposed Satluj's removal from ZEE5 in India and plans community screenings of the film. It will also hold educational seminars on Jaswant Singh Khalra, saying his story must remain accessible.

general news.headline: Trump declares Iran ceasefire is over, calls its leaders sick, bunch of scumWeeks after signing an interim peace deal to end the war, Donald Trump said the US-Iran ceasefire was over as the countries traded strikes overnight. The US unleashed a new wave of military strikes on Iran after it claimed Tehran hit three ships in Hormuz.

Sensex ends 1,677 points lower: 3 reasons why stock market crashed todayAs of 1:56 pm, selling pressure intensified on Dalal Street, with the benchmark BSE Sensex tumbling 1,122.46 points, or 1.44%, to 77,058.26, while the NSE Nifty50 plunged 342.60 points, or 1.40%, to 24,056.10 as escalating US-Iran tensions, a sharp rise in crude oil prices and weak global cues triggered broad-based risk-off sentiment across equities.

Mocked for feasting on kachori, noodles as Wangchuk fasts, CJP's Dipke clarifiesAs activist Sonam Wangchuk's indefinite fast at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi completed its 10th day on Tuesday, the spotlight shifted from the protest to the plate of the Cockroach Janta Party's Abhijeet Dipke. This is what he had to say when asked why he was feasting even as Wangchuk fasted.

Congress occupied 24 Akbar Road headquarters without authorisation since 2013: RTIThe RTI reply, furnished by the Directorate of Estates, which falls under the Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, states that Bungalow No. 24, Akbar Road was allotted to the Congress party on August 7, 1992. However, its allotment was cancelled with effect from June 26, 2013.

Is FIFA favouring Argentina? Egypt coach's rigged World Cup charge divides opinionFIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan accused officials of rigging his side's dramatic 2-3 defeat to Argentina after the defending champions staged a stunning late comeback in Atlanta. His allegations drew contrasting reactions from Thierry Henry, Roy Keane and Zlatan Ibrahimovic, reigniting the debate over VAR consistency and refereeing standards.

New EPFO portal is here. What does it mean for your PF account?EPFO has given its digital platform a major upgrade, bringing all key PF services under one roof. But how will this benefit crores of EPF members? Let's have a look.

Trudeau ghost exorcised? No proof of India govt link in Nijjar killing, says CanadaCanada's RCMP has said it found no evidence connecting the Indian government to the killing of Khalistani terrorist Hardeep Sigh Nijjar. This comes nearly three years after then-Canadian PM Justin Trudeau alleged a "potential link", throwing bilateral ties into a deep chill.

Who was KPS Gill, the controversial supercop, and what was Punjab '95?After three decades, the life of KPS Gill, the Supercop hailed for helping end militancy in Punjab and Assam but always shrouded in controversy, is back in the spotlight. The reason is Diljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj, earlier named Panjab '95, and the sudden removal of the movie from ZEE5. Who was Gill, and what happened in Punjab in 1995?

Bengaluru traffic in a 30-minute standoff with a cobra that wouldn't moveA cobra stopped in the middle of Bengaluru's Palace Road, holding up traffic for nearly 30 minutes. The viral video drew several reactions online.

Maharashtra: 15 feared trapped as building collapses near Pune amid heavy rainAuthorities said efforts are underway to clear the rubble using heavy machinery while emergency responders are attempting to reach those trapped.

US gangs and Punjab cops: Why FBI wants SHO extradited as part of OP Hard BallUS authorities have said they will seek the extradition of Gurinderjit Singh, the SHO of Tanda police station in Punjab's Hoshiarpur district, after charging him with allegedly extorting $400,000 from a Los Angeles-based family by framing their relatives in India in a fake murder case.

Bengal: Key accused in Baruipur minor rape-murder killed in police encounterThe accused, identified as Prabash Mondal, was among three people arrested in connection with the rape and murder of the girl, whose body was found stuffed inside a sack in a pond in Baruipur earlier this week, triggering widespread outrage and violent protests in the area.

Gurgaon's 'Venice moment' leaves vehicles, residents battling waterlogged roadsHeavy monsoon showers flooded several parts of Gurgaon, with residents sharing videos of submerged streets and struggling commuters. The visuals sparked jokes about the city's "Venice moment" even as they renewed focus on the city's recurring drainage problems.

Name one car damaged by ethanol fuel: Nitin Gadkari dares E20 criticsUnion Minister Nitin Gadkari defended the E20 petrol rollout and challenged critics to name any damaged car. His remarks came as concerns over mileage, older vehicles and a possible move beyond E20 intensified.

India clears first plant-based Vitamin D3 for supplements and foodsThe FSSAI has approved India's first plant-sourced vitamin D3 for supplements, fortified foods and nutraceuticals. The move widens options for vegetarian and vegan consumers as vitamin D deficiency remains common.

Microsoft is offering 9 months of salary for free to some laid-off employeesMicrosoft just announced fresh layoffs affecting around 4,800 employees globally, but many affected workers in the US will receive some severance benefits. In some cases, employees could get up to 39 weeks of base pay, along with stock vesting and healthcare support.
Aamir Khan-Gauri Spratt wore Sabyasachi outfits for their wedding. New picAfter DeepVeer, NickYanka, and Virushka, meet the newest Sabyasachi couple: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt.

MAGA playbook: Iran President on Trump's intervention in US's FIFA red card rowIranian President Masoud Pezeshkian attacked the US over the FIFA World Cup red card controversy and likened its conduct to Donald Trump's MAGA playbook. His remarks came minutes after Trump said Washington's interim peace agreement with Tehran was over.

The 35°C wet bulb limit: How humid heat can kill a healthy humanA wet bulb temperature of 35 degrees Celsius has long been called the limit of human survival, the point where sweat can no longer cool the body. New research reveals the death threshold is far lower, and parts of South Asia are already dangerously close.

Who gained from Satluj's 48-hour run on ZEE5? 5 big questions about Diljit filmSatluj briefly appeared on ZEE5 India before being taken down within 48 hours. The episode has raised questions over censorship, OTT rules, piracy and the film's sudden reach. Here are five big questions regarding Satluj and the developments that followed that need to be raised and answered.

Who plays whom in Satluj? Your guide to people that inspired film's charactersSatluj has renewed interest in the real people behind its characters after its brief Zee5 release. The film revisits Jaswant Singh Khalra's case while viewers track how closely each role mirrors real events.

Bengaluru woman's vigorous workout on park gym machine raises safety concernsThe 10-second video, shared by Bengaluru-based X user Sandeep Parswanath, shows the elderly woman using an air swing machine with vigorous back-and-forth motion that appears sped up.

Why Kerala called Wayanad landslide man-made, not natural disasterA landslide hit the Kalladi tunnel construction site in rain-hit Wayanad, killing three workers and trapping others. The Kerala government called it a man-made disaster, blaming it on excavated soil that was kept near the project site.

Buck stops with the Sangh: Why it can't escape Ram Mandir theft responsibilityThe Ram Mandir donation theft case has turned out to be, perhaps, the stiffest test for the Sangh Parivar. The RSS cannot simultaneously claim moral ownership of the Ram Mandir movement during its hour of triumph and distance itself from institutional failures during a crisis.

sports.headline: MAGA playbook: Iran President on Trump's intervention in US's FIFA red card row Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian attacked the US over the FIFA World Cup red card controversy and likened its conduct to Donald Trump's MAGA playbook. His remarks came minutes after Trump said Washington's interim peace agreement with Tehran was over.

Afghanistan mourn Shapoor Zadran, postpone domestic T20 league as mark of respect The Afghanistan Cricket Board postponed two days of the Shpageeza Cricket League as a mark of respect following the death of former pacer Shapoor Zadran at the age of 38. The Afghanistan cricket fraternity will gather in Kabul to bid farewell to one of the pioneers of the country's rise in world cricket.

Drop Tilak. Bring back Sanju Samson? Ex-coach warns India after 3rd T20I surrender Abhishek Nayar has advised India not to reshuffle the XI merely to recall Sanju Samson after the third T20I loss in Nottingham. He said Samson should return only in the top three as India reflect on form and conditions.

Archer fires subtle dig at IPL over easy pitches: Back to normal in England England pacer Jofra Archer believes bowling in English conditions is a return to "normal" after spending the IPL on "easy wickets and smaller boundaries" in India, saying bowlers have a much bigger margin for error back home.

Nice to play 90 mins: Djokovic's cheeky Messi quip is viral after Wimbledon marathon After a historic five-hour Wimbledon marathon, Novak Djokovic delivered a cheeky nod to fellow 39-year-old icon Lionel Messi, joking that he envies football's strict 90-minute limit after enduring his own open-ended epic.

Is FIFA favouring Argentina? Egypt coach's rigged World Cup charge divides opinion FIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan accused officials of rigging his side's dramatic 2-3 defeat to Argentina after the defending champions staged a stunning late comeback in Atlanta. His allegations drew contrasting reactions from Thierry Henry, Roy Keane and Zlatan Ibrahimovic, reigniting the debate over VAR consistency and refereeing standards.

I'll never watch World Cup again: Egypt coach says FIFA favoured Messi Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan launched an extraordinary attack on FIFA after his side's dramatic FIFA World Cup Round of 16 defeat to Argentina, alleging that the governing body wanted Lionel Messi and the defending champions to remain in the tournament.

Egypt coach booked after anti-racism gesture in World Cup loss to Argentina Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan found himself at the centre of controversy after making a gesture towards the match officials during his side's FIFA World Cup Round of 16 loss to Argentina.

Two 39-year-olds, Messi and Djokovic, prove it's never over till it's over An epic Wimbledon quarter-final. An unforgettable World Cup comeback. On the same Tuesday, Novak Djokovic and Lionel Messi, both 39, produced performances that defied age, expectation and the temptation to believe the story was already over.

Wimbledon: Djokovic likens marathon quarters win to iconic 2019 final vs Federer Novak Djokovic said his record-breaking Wimbledon quarter-final victory over Felix Auger-Aliassime ranks among the finest matches of his career, comparing the five-hour, 15-minute epic to his unforgettable 2019 final against Roger Federer.

Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach first quarter-final since 1954 Switzerland reached their first World Cup quarter-final since 1954 after edging Colombia 4-3 on penalties following a goalless draw. Gregor Kobel starred before Ruben Vargas converted the decisive kick to set up a last-eight clash with Argentina.

It was atrocious: Shreyas Iyer shuns cliches after India slip to new lowShreyas Iyer did not hide behind the usual cricketing cliches after India's humiliating 125-run defeat to England in the third T20I. Calling the performance "atrocious", the India captain delivered one of the most brutally honest post-match assessments in recent memory.

Wimbledon: Djokovic survives five-hour epic to set up semifinal clash vs SinnerNovak Djokovic survived the longest Wimbledon quarter-final in history, defeating Felix Auger-Aliassime in a five-hour, 15-minute thriller to reach a record eighth straight semi-final, where he will face defending champion Jannik Sinner.

Tears, triumph and a tribute: Lionel Messi's unforgettable night in AtlantaLionel Messi was overcome with emotion after Argentina's stunning comeback win over Egypt in the World Cup Round of 16. From tears at the final whistle to teammates lifting him in celebration, the unforgettable scenes captured the spirit of the defending champions.

Is this the last of Sanju Samson in T20Is? Gambhir clarifies shock selection callGautam Gambhir has insisted Sanju Samson remains in India's T20I plans despite his surprise omission against England. The India coach said selection is based on form and results, while leaving the door open for the World Cup hero to make a comeback.

World Cup rigged for Argentina: Egypt rage at VAR-fuelled comebackFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina beat Egypt 3-2 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals after VAR disallowed Mostafa Ziko's goal. The decision, and a later non-review before Enzo Fernandez's winner, left Egypt raging over inconsistency.

India hit new low under Gambhir, slump to worst-ever T20I defeatIndia were blown away by England in the 3rd T20I of the series. The Shreyas Iyer side was bowled out for just 76 runs, India's 2nd lowest total in the history of T20Is. With the win, England take an unassailable lead of 2-0 in the 5-match series.

Dinesh Karthik loses cool after India send Harshit Rana to bat in powerplayFormer India cricketer Dinesh Karthik absolutely lost it at the Indian team management during India's 3rd T20I match against England. Karthik was left seething after Harshit Rana was sent to bat in the powerplay ahead of Shivam Dube.

SUI 4-3 COL Highlights: Switzerland into maiden QFs, beat Colombia on penaltiesSwitzerland vs Colombia Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Switzerland held their nerve to beat Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless draw, reaching the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954. Goalkeeper Gregor Kobel starred in the shootout as Murat Yakin's side made history.

International Olympic Committee provisionally lifts suspension of RussiaThe IOC has provisionally lifted the suspension of the Russian Olympic Committee after it removed sports bodies from occupied Ukrainian territories. The Olympic body stressed the move does not alter its condemnation of Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

Messi magic masks Argentina's flaws in epic comeback win over EgyptFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina came from two goals down to beat Egypt 3-2 in Atlanta after Messi drove the fightback. The escape sent the holders into the quarter-finals but also exposed their reliance on the captain.

Wimbledon: Jannik Sinner digs deep to reach semis, outsmarts big-serving StruffJannik Sinner beat Jan-Lennard Struff in straight sets and Coco Gauff came from a set down to defeat Jessica Pegula and reach the Wimbledon semi-finals. Struff earned a standing ovation for his landmark run, while Gauff broke new ground with her first last-four appearance here.

ARG 3-2 EGY Highlights: Lionel Messi leads Argentina's sensational comeback win in R16Argentina (ARG) vs Egypt (EGY) Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: (ARG 3-2 EGY) Argentina produce one of the comebacks of the tournament, recovering from 2-0 down to win 3-2. Lionel Messi inspires the revival before Enzo Fernandez completes the dramatic turnaround in stoppage time.

Days after viral prank, Djokovic reunites with ball girl, gifts her signed capNovak Djokovic turned a viral Wimbledon prank into a heartwarming moment by reuniting with the ball girl he had jokingly startled during his second-round match. The Serbian apologised with a smile, gifted her a signed Wimbledon cap, and shared a light-hearted exchange that has won over tennis fans.

QF Highlights: Novak Djokovic defies the odds to beat Auger-AliassimeWimbledon 2026 Highlights: Novak Djokovic and Felix Auger-Aliassime are in action in the men's quarterfinal match. Djokovic is battling a calf injury, but has been able to hold up to the Canadian youngster. Both players have won two sets each and the game will now be decided by the fifth and final set of the match.

Happy Birthday captain cool: Thalapathy Vijay's heartfelt wish for Thala DhoniTamil Nadu Chief Minister Joseph Vijay wished MS Dhoni on his birthday and praised his leadership, humility and bond with the state. The tribute underlined how deeply the former India captain remains woven into Tamil Nadu's sporting identity.

Shubman Gill plays cricket with Antonelli in Mercedes garage at British GP. VideoShubman Gill played corridor cricket with Kimi Antonelli inside the Mercedes garage at Silverstone. The crossover video linked cricket and Formula One before Gill's ODI tour of England.

Shapoor Zadran, former Afghanistan fast bowler, dies at 38 after prolonged illnessFormer Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran died at 38 in New Delhi following a prolonged battle with a rare immune disorder, leaving the global cricketing fraternity mourning one of the pioneers of Afghanistan cricket.

Messi, Salah set for World Cup showdown as Argentina face EgyptLionel Messi and Mohamed Salah will go head-to-head on Tuesday when Argentina and Egypt meet in the Round of 16, and the World Cup has handed us exactly the kind of match it needed. A place in the quarter-finals is on the line. So is a piece of individual history.

Ronaldo, you'll always be my inspiration: Siraj pens emotional noteFIFA World Cup 2026: Mohammed Siraj posted an emotional tribute to Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's World Cup exit against Spain. The message captured how Ronaldo's reach extends beyond football, even as debate over his unfinished World Cup story continued.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
